config.name: PairCorrectly
config.description: Matched-pair round-robin across the four archetype-bot pairs: bulwark-control+fortress, ember-aggro+hellfire, hollow-disruption+reaper, tide-thorn-combo+wavebreaker. Cross-pair only (no mirror). Knob 3 (harness integrity-delta tiebreaker) is active, so a cap-hit game whose Conduits are unevenly damaged becomes a decisive result by the integrity side. ENGINE STATUS (2026-04-20): the v1 interpreter wires raw Force -> Conduit damage and atomic DealDamage, but Fortification, Sentinel, Fortify, Phantom, Shroud, and several other keyword macros are declared in CCGNF but not yet dispatched at runtime -- see docs/plan/engine-completion-guide.md. Numbers in this bench are interpretable relative to the 12.0 baseline, but an unknown fraction of card text is currently cosmetic. Baseline artifact: ai-testing-data/PairCorrectly.baseline.results.json. RUNNING AND ANALYSING: `make bench-run TOURNAMENT=PairCorrectly` posts the config and prints a summary (REST must be running; `make rest` in another shell with CCGNF_AI_EDITOR=1). `make bench-summary RESULTS=<path> [BASELINE=<path>] [MATCHUPS=1]` re-summarises any saved JSON. The underlying scripts are tools/bench-summarize.py and tools/bench-matrix.py -- don't reach for inline Python, these handle the analyses we actually use.

notes: Close race: 'TiThWave' edges out runner-up by 8 points — consider more games per matchup.

learningLog: Tournament 'PairCorrectly' ran 240 games across 6 matchup(s). Pairs: BulFort=(deck:bulwark-control,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-fortress), EmbHell=(deck:ember-aggro,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire), HolReap=(deck:hollow-disruption,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-reaper), TiThWave=(deck:tide-thorn-combo,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker)

Matchup BulFort vs EmbHell: deck 'bulwark-control' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-fortress' versus deck 'ember-aggro' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire'. EmbHell won 28/40 (AWins:12,Draws:0, A side 30%). Different deck + bot, close outcome — either both pairs are balanced or the dominant factor cancels out. Follow up with a controlled matchup. Average game length: 196 steps.

Matchup BulFort vs HolReap: deck 'bulwark-control' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-fortress' versus deck 'hollow-disruption' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-reaper'. BulFort won 40/40 (BWins:0,Draws:0, 100%). Different deck + bot: the (experimental/2026-04-19-fortress, bulwark-control) pair wins — hard to attribute to either axis alone; follow up with a controlled same-bot or same-deck matchup to isolate. Average game length: 134 steps.

Matchup BulFort vs TiThWave: deck 'bulwark-control' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-fortress' versus deck 'tide-thorn-combo' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker'. TiThWave won 22/40 (AWins:18,Draws:0, A side 45%). Different deck + bot, close outcome — either both pairs are balanced or the dominant factor cancels out. Follow up with a controlled matchup. Average game length: 182 steps.

Matchup EmbHell vs HolReap: deck 'ember-aggro' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire' versus deck 'hollow-disruption' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-reaper'. EmbHell won 40/40 (BWins:0,Draws:0, 100%). Different deck + bot: the (experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire, ember-aggro) pair wins — hard to attribute to either axis alone; follow up with a controlled same-bot or same-deck matchup to isolate. Average game length: 156 steps.

Matchup EmbHell vs TiThWave: deck 'ember-aggro' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire' versus deck 'tide-thorn-combo' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker'. TiThWave won 28/40 (AWins:12,Draws:0, A side 30%). Different deck + bot, close outcome — either both pairs are balanced or the dominant factor cancels out. Follow up with a controlled matchup. Average game length: 155 steps.

Matchup HolReap vs TiThWave: deck 'hollow-disruption' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-reaper' versus deck 'tide-thorn-combo' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker'. TiThWave won 40/40 (AWins:0,Draws:0, A side 0%). Different deck + bot: the (experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker, tide-thorn-combo) pair wins — hard to attribute to either axis alone; follow up with a controlled same-bot or same-deck matchup to isolate. Average game length: 118 steps.

Across all matchups, 'TiThWave' (deck:tide-thorn-combo,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker) outperformed 'HolReap' (deck:hollow-disruption,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-reaper). Pattern: the top pair's bot profile is worth studying as a candidate to pair with similar decks; the weakest pair suggests either a weight-table mismatch or a deck that disagrees with the bot's scoring priorities.
Observation: Close race: 'TiThWave' edges out runner-up by 8 points — consider more games per matchup.

LEARNING HINT: When choosing a bot for a new deck, prefer profiles whose matched decks in this tournament share archetype tags (aggro/control/midrange) with the new deck. Avoid pairing a utility bot tuned for defence with an aggro deck — it will underweight tempo plays.
